text_blocks.text: The motif of the England national football
team has three lions passant guardant, the emblem of
King Richard I, who reigned from 1189 to 1199. The
lions, often blue, have had minor changes to colour
and appearance. Initially topped by a crown, this was
removed in 1949 when the FA was given an official
coat of arms by the College of Arms; this introduced
ten Tudor roses, one for each of the regional branches
of the FA. Since 2003, England top their logo with
a star to recognise their World Cup win in 1966; this
10as first embroidered onto the left sleeve of the home
kit, and a year later was moved to its current position,
